project.name: konva-motion
project.description: Remotion-style timeline-driven animation for Konva. A Composition (Konva.Stage) owns a frame clock and walks range-gated Sequence layers each tick, with built-in flexbox layout via flexily.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Working notes for Claude Code agents, documenting the repo's mental model (Composition/Sequence frame clock), monorepo layout, conventions, common commands, and gotchas.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview introducing konva-motion's timeline-driven animation model with a runnable Composition/Sequence example, package table, quick-start commands, and doc links.

nodes.name: biome.json
nodes.summary: Root Biome configuration for lint and format across the workspace, including VCS integration, import organization, linter, and formatter rules.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Root workspace manifest defining the pnpm package manager pin, dev dependencies (Biome, TypeScript), and scripts to build packages, run the demo, and check/format with Biome.

nodes.name: pnpm-workspace.yaml
nodes.summary: pnpm workspace definition enumerating the workspace package globs (packages/* and demo) that participate in workspace:* linking.

nodes.name: tsconfig.base.json
nodes.summary: Shared TypeScript compiler base extended by per-package tsconfigs, enabling strict mode, composite project builds, ES2022/ESNext targets, and declaration/source maps.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses composite: true to enable tsc -b project-reference incremental builds across the monorepo.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: Root solution-style TypeScript config with no files of its own, referencing the core, layout, timeline, and demo subprojects for orchestrated tsc -b builds.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: HTML entry document for the KmStudio demo app; mounts a #root div, preloads Google Fonts, and bootstraps the Vite React app via /src/main.tsx.
nodes.languageNotes: Standard Vite single-page-app shell: an empty mount node plus a type="module" script tag pointing at the TSX entry.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Package manifest for the demo workspace; declares Vite dev/build/preview scripts and depends on the @konva-motion/core and @konva-motion/timeline workspace packages alongside konva, React, react-router-dom, and zod.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses pnpm workspace protocol (workspace:*) to link the local @konva-motion/* packages; konva is pinned to a real version here per the peer-dependency convention.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript configuration for the demo, extending the repo base config with noEmit type-checking only, react-jsx, and project references to the core and timeline packages.
nodes.languageNotes: composite:false + noEmit:true means this config is for typechecking only — Vite performs the actual build.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Primary usage documentation for konva-motion covering installation, the Composition/Sequence API, interpolation and easing helpers, how-to animation recipes, Flex layout, the Text and Audio components, and server-side offline rendering.
nodes.languageNotes: Reference doc structured around the public API surface of @konva-motion/core; the target API examples must stay in sync with the package's exports.

nodes.name: architecture.md
nodes.summary: Design document describing the engine architecture: the core vs timeline package split, the per-frame tick loop that gates and redraws sequences, teardown, and singleton enforcement.

nodes.name: contributing.md
nodes.summary: Contributor guide covering local setup, common pnpm commands, and the monorepo layout for the konva-motion workspace.

nodes.name: launch.json
nodes.summary: Claude Code launch configuration declaring debug/run configurations for the workspace.

nodes.name: settings.json
nodes.summary: Claude Code settings enabling project plugins for the konva-motion workspace.

nodes.name: .npmrc
nodes.summary: npm/pnpm registry and resolution configuration for the monorepo.

nodes.name: assets.d.ts
nodes.summary: Ambient TypeScript module declarations letting the demo import media assets (images, audio, video) as URL strings via Vite.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses `declare module '*.ext'` ambient declarations so Vite asset imports typecheck.

nodes.name: audio-mixer.ts
nodes.summary: Demo that visualizes a multi-track audio mixer, animating volume bars and channel strips driven by reactive mixer state from the core Composition.

nodes.name: timecode
nodes.summary: Formats a frame number into a zero-padded MM:SS timecode string.

nodes.name: basic.ts
nodes.summary: Minimal introductory demo animating a circle's position and a square's opacity across two sequences.

nodes.name: bouncing.ts
nodes.summary: Demo animating a bouncing ball with vertical motion and squash-and-stretch scaling.

nodes.name: cohabit.ts
nodes.summary: Large production-style promotional video demo orchestrating video clips, music, voiceover, SFX, captions, and a logo reveal across many timed sequences.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of local builder helpers (addClip/addMusic/addVo/addCaption) to compose a long timeline.

nodes.name: fadeRise
nodes.summary: Computes a combined fade-in/fade-out opacity and upward rise offset for a node based on the current frame.

nodes.name: applyGrade
nodes.summary: Applies a color-grade overlay (tint, vignette, blend layers) to a sequence.

nodes.name: addClip
nodes.summary: Creates a video clip sequence with grade, volume ducking, and entrance scale/offset animation.

nodes.name: addMusic
nodes.summary: Adds a music track sequence with volume curve and ducking against voiceover.

nodes.name: addVo
nodes.summary: Adds a voiceover audio sequence with fade-based volume envelope.

nodes.name: addCaption
nodes.summary: Creates an animated caption text node with fade and rise entrance within a sequence.

nodes.name: makeBubble
nodes.summary: Builds a rounded chat-bubble group sized to its text content.

nodes.name: makeLogo
nodes.summary: Constructs the branded logo group (windows mark plus wordmark) at a given scale and color.

nodes.name: colors.ts
nodes.summary: Demo animating color interpolation across background, concentric rings, and a pulsing blob.

nodes.name: easings.ts
nodes.summary: Demo comparing easing curves by animating dots across the stage with different easing functions.

nodes.name: flex-layout.ts
nodes.summary: Demo showing the built-in flexbox layout reflowing a card whose width animates each frame.

nodes.name: flex-row-grow.ts
nodes.summary: Demo of a flex row where child grow factors animate, reflowing image widths over time.

nodes.name: flex-showcase.ts
nodes.summary: Comprehensive flexbox showcase demonstrating justify, align, grow, and gap behaviors with animated swatches.

nodes.name: swatch
nodes.summary: Factory creating a colored Block swatch of a given size for use in flex rows.

nodes.name: flex-typewriter.ts
nodes.summary: Demo combining flex layout with a typewriter text reveal inside an auto-sizing card.

nodes.name: ig-story.ts
nodes.summary: Instagram-story style demo rendering multiple animated scenes with hero images, word reveals, stickers, and captions.

nodes.name: buildScene
nodes.summary: Builds a single story scene sequence with background, hero, animated words, stickers, and caption, wiring per-frame updaters.

nodes.name: image-clip.ts
nodes.summary: Demo clipping an image to an animated circular mask with expanding rings.

nodes.name: loadImage
nodes.summary: Loads an image URL into an HTMLImageElement, resolving a promise on load.

nodes.name: image-fade.ts
nodes.summary: Demo cross-fading between images with scale and offset transitions.

nodes.name: loadImage
nodes.summary: Loads an image URL into an HTMLImageElement, resolving a promise on load.

nodes.name: image-slider.ts
nodes.summary: Demo sliding between images horizontally with eased transitions and a frame label.

nodes.name: loadImage
nodes.summary: Loads an image URL into an HTMLImageElement, resolving a promise on load.

nodes.name: journey.ts
nodes.summary: Cinematic multi-chapter journey demo with hero clips, polaroids, titles, a progress bar, and timecode overlay.

nodes.name: formatTimecode
nodes.summary: Formats the current frame and total into a MM:SS / MM:SS timecode readout string.

nodes.name: keyframes.ts
nodes.summary: Demo driving a star through multi-stop keyframed position, rotation, scale, and color animations.

nodes.name: staggered.ts
nodes.summary: Demo animating a palette of cards into view with a staggered, sequence-offset entrance.

nodes.name: text-fit.ts
nodes.summary: Demo showcasing text auto-fitting behaviors inside boxes whose dimensions animate.

nodes.name: label
nodes.summary: Creates a small caption Text node at a given position.

nodes.name: text-highlight.ts
nodes.summary: Demo highlighting substrings within text blocks, animating highlight ranges and box sizing.

nodes.name: range
nodes.summary: Computes the start/end character indices of a needle substring within a haystack.

nodes.name: text-typewriter.ts
nodes.summary: Demo revealing text character-by-character in a typewriter effect across several columns and bubbles.

nodes.name: transforms.ts
nodes.summary: Demo animating rotation and scale transforms on a ring of shapes plus a radial burst flash.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Shared TypeScript type definitions for demo entries, declaring the DemoDef contract (build function, props schema) used across all studio demos.

nodes.name: typewriter.ts
nodes.summary: Demo that builds a chat-style typewriter animation by staggering Text/Block bubbles across sequences on a Composition.

nodes.name: video-sync.ts
nodes.summary: Demo showcasing frame-synced video playback by laying out two Video sequences and reflecting the composition's play/frame state into status overlays.

nodes.name: main.tsx
nodes.summary: Browser entry point that mounts the studio App into the DOM via React createRoot wrapped in a HashRouter, and loads global studio styles.

nodes.name: node-module-shim.ts
nodes.summary: Browser shim providing a no-op createRequire so Node 'module' imports resolve harmlessly in the Vite build.

nodes.name: createRequire
nodes.summary: No-op stand-in for Node's createRequire that returns a throwing require, satisfying bundler resolution in the browser.

nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: Studio root component wiring routing, composition lifecycle, keyboard shortcuts, fullscreen/zoom state, and the player/sidebar/right-panel layout.

nodes.name: StageStatus
nodes.summary: Overlay component that subscribes to a composition's signals to render the current stage title and dimensions.

nodes.name: Studio
nodes.summary: Main studio screen managing the active demo's composition, prop editing, viewport fit/zoom, keyboard transport shortcuts, and panel visibility.

nodes.name: StudioRoute
nodes.summary: Route wrapper that resolves the demo id from URL params via findDemo and renders the Studio screen.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Top-level router component defining the studio routes and default redirect to the first demo.

nodes.name: catalog.ts
nodes.summary: Central demo registry that imports every demo module, attaches metadata and schemas, and exports grouped/flat catalogs plus a findDemo lookup.

nodes.name: entry
nodes.summary: Helper that combines a demo definition with metadata to produce a fully-formed StudioDemo catalog entry.

nodes.name: Icon.tsx
nodes.summary: SVG icon component rendering a named glyph from an internal path registry, used throughout the studio UI.

nodes.name: Icon
nodes.summary: Renders an inline SVG for the given icon name at the requested size with optional style overrides.

nodes.name: kf.ts
nodes.summary: Schema-builder DSL wrapping Zod with UI metadata (kf.text/number/color/select/object/array) plus helpers to derive defaults and merge prop values.
nodes.languageNotes: Attaches UI metadata to Zod schemas via Object.defineProperty so the form renderer can introspect field kinds.

nodes.name: tag
nodes.summary: Attaches a non-enumerable UI metadata object to a Zod schema and records its describe() string.

nodes.name: kmDefault
nodes.summary: Recursively derives a default value object from a schema, cloning array item defaults as needed.

nodes.name: kmMerge
nodes.summary: Deep-merges an override value tree onto a base default tree, recursing into nested objects.

nodes.name: Player.tsx
nodes.summary: Transport bar component providing play/pause, scrubbing, time display, loop, volume/mute, and fullscreen controls bound to a composition's signals.

nodes.name: fmt
nodes.summary: Formats a seconds value into a zero-padded mm:ss timecode string.

nodes.name: Player
nodes.summary: Transport UI that subscribes to composition frame/playing signals and drives seek, scrub-drag, loop, mute, and volume controls.

nodes.name: RightPanel.tsx
nodes.summary: Collapsible right-side studio panel with Props, Info, and Code tabs, hosting the schema-driven prop editor for the active demo.

nodes.name: PropsTab
nodes.summary: Tab that renders the demo's schema form and a reset control, forwarding value changes upward.

nodes.name: InfoTab
nodes.summary: Tab presenting demo metadata such as description, tags, fps, and computed duration in seconds.

nodes.name: CodeTab
nodes.summary: Tab displaying the demo's source/usage code snippet for reference.

nodes.name: RightPanel
nodes.summary: Container that renders the tab strip and the active tab body, with a close affordance.

nodes.name: PanelHandle
nodes.summary: Edge handle button that reopens the right panel when it is collapsed.

nodes.name: SchemaForm.tsx
nodes.summary: Schema-driven form renderer that introspects kf/Zod metadata to render typed field editors (text, number, color, select, object, array) with drag-reorder support.
nodes.languageNotes: Dispatches on per-field UI metadata to render a tree of specialized field components, including recursive object/array nesting.

nodes.name: Dropdown
nodes.summary: Custom popover dropdown with outside-click dismissal used by select-style fields.

nodes.name: FieldShell
nodes.summary: Layout wrapper rendering a field's label, description, and child control.

nodes.name: KNumber
nodes.summary: Numeric field editor with min/max handling that emits parsed number changes.

nodes.name: KColor
nodes.summary: Color field editor offering swatches and validated hex input.

nodes.name: KBool
nodes.summary: Boolean toggle field editor emitting on/off changes.

nodes.name: KMultiselect
nodes.summary: Multi-select field editor toggling membership in an array of enum values.

nodes.name: KObject
nodes.summary: Collapsible group field that recursively renders nested object schema fields.

nodes.name: KArray
nodes.summary: Array field editor supporting add/remove, drag-to-reorder, and per-item recursive field rendering.

nodes.name: InlineColor
nodes.summary: Compact inline color editor variant for use inside array rows.

nodes.name: Field
nodes.summary: Dispatcher that selects the appropriate field editor component based on a schema node's UI kind.

nodes.name: SchemaForm
nodes.summary: Top-level form component that renders the root schema's fields and bubbles value changes.

nodes.name: Sidebar.tsx
nodes.summary: Left navigation listing demos grouped by category with search filtering, collapsible groups, and active-item selection.

nodes.name: Sidebar
nodes.summary: Renders the grouped, searchable demo list and invokes onSelect when a demo is chosen.

nodes.name: styles.css
nodes.summary: Global stylesheet for the entire studio UI, defining layout, theming, controls, panels, and player styling.

nodes.name: useComposition.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that builds, hosts, and tears down a demo's Composition, exposes a props signal, and refreshes the stage on prop changes.

nodes.name: createSignal
nodes.summary: Minimal observable signal factory with get/set/subscribe semantics used to feed live props into a composition.

nodes.name: useComposition
nodes.summary: Hook that constructs a demo's composition in a hidden host element, wires a props signal to refresh, and disposes it on unmount or demo change.

nodes.name: useSignal.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that subscribes a component to a core ReadonlySignal via useSyncExternalStore for tear-free reads.

nodes.name: useSignal
nodes.summary: Subscribes to a signal's value through useSyncExternalStore and returns the current value.

nodes.name: ZoomControl.tsx
nodes.summary: Dropdown control for selecting stage zoom level (fit or discrete percentage steps) for the studio viewport.

nodes.name: ZoomControl
nodes.summary: Popover that lists zoom presets and emits the selected zoom mode/percentage.

nodes.name: vite.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vite build configuration for the demo app, enabling the React plugin and any asset/alias settings.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Package manifest for @konva-motion/core declaring ESM exports pointing at dist/, build scripts, flexily dependency, and konva as a peer dependency.

nodes.name: asset.ts
nodes.summary: Type definitions describing collected audio asset descriptors emitted during offline rendering.

nodes.name: audio-driver.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the AudioDriver interface and driver context, the abstraction implemented by preview and rendering audio drivers.

nodes.name: audio-for-preview.ts
nodes.summary: Preview-mode audio driver that plays/pauses and seeks a live audio source in sync with the composition's play state and frame clock.

nodes.name: PreviewAudioDriver
nodes.summary: AudioDriver implementation that keeps a real audio element synced to the composition playhead, correcting drift and toggling playback with the transport.

nodes.name: audio-for-rendering.ts
nodes.summary: Rendering-mode audio driver that mutes live playback and instead collects audio asset timing for offline/server rendering.

nodes.name: RenderingAudioDriver
nodes.summary: AudioDriver for offline rendering that mutes the source and records audio asset descriptors per frame instead of playing audio.

nodes.name: audio-source-browser.ts
nodes.summary: Browser AudioSource backed by an HTMLAudioElement, handling load/ready, debounced seeking, play/pause, volume, and mute.
nodes.languageNotes: Serializes overlapping seeks through a pending-resolver queue to avoid thrashing the media element's seeked events.

nodes.name: BrowserAudioSource
nodes.summary: HTMLAudioElement-backed AudioSource implementation managing ready callbacks, queued seeks, and playback controls for the browser.

nodes.name: audio-source.ts
nodes.summary: AudioSource interface and SeekMode definition that abstract audio playback backends across browser and rendering environments.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the Audio node (extends a Konva container) that plays an audio source range-gated to its sequence, wiring playback to a per-composition AudioMixer and an environment-specific driver (preview vs rendering).
nodes.languageNotes: Lazily resolves the owning Composition via getStage()/getComposition() and selects a driver based on detectEnvironment().

nodes.name: Audio
nodes.summary: Konva-group-derived audio node: loads a source, registers with the composition mixer, ticks an AudioDriver each frame, and applies muted/volume from both its own signals and the mixer.

nodes.name: isAudioNode
nodes.summary: Type guard that detects whether a Konva node is an Audio node via its AUDIO_MARK attribute.

nodes.name: mixer.ts
nodes.summary: AudioMixer: a per-composition master volume/mute bus that registered audio channels subscribe to and reapply when master settings change.

nodes.name: AudioMixer
nodes.summary: Master audio bus holding volume/muted signals and a set of channels; register/unregister bind channels, and setVolume/setMuted reapply to all registered channels.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Type definitions for audio configuration (AudioConfig) consumed by the Audio node.

nodes.name: block.ts
nodes.summary: Block layout primitive (extends a Konva group) that renders a flex-laid-out box with a background rect supporting solid/gradient fills, per-edge borders, corner radius, and shadows.

nodes.name: Block
nodes.summary: Konva-group-derived container that owns a background Rect and recomputes its flex layout plus background styling on size/attr changes.

nodes.name: applyBackground
nodes.summary: Applies a background value to a Rect, resolving solid colors as well as linear and radial gradient color stops and geometry.

nodes.name: applyBorder
nodes.summary: Configures stroke width, color, and optional dashed style on a Rect for border rendering.

nodes.name: applyShadow
nodes.summary: Enables and configures shadow color, blur, offset, and opacity on a Rect, or disables shadow when absent.

nodes.name: composition.ts
nodes.summary: Heart of the engine: Composition (extends Konva.Stage) owns the frame clock (fps, durationInFrames, signals) and drives a RAF tick loop, while Sequence (extends Konva.Layer) is a range-gated layer that runs updaters and batchDraws while the playhead is inside its window.
nodes.languageNotes: Reads requestAnimationFrame/performance.now off globalThis with fallbacks so importing in Node is safe; play() requires a browser, setFrame()/renderFrame() work offline.

nodes.name: Composition
nodes.summary: Konva.Stage subclass that maintains frame/isPlaying signals, an AudioMixer, an event emitter, and a RAF-driven clock; exposes play/pause/stop/setFrame/renderFrame plus delayRender/continueRender for offline rendering.

nodes.name: Sequence
nodes.summary: Konva.Layer subclass gated to a [from, from+durationInFrames) frame window; toggles visibility, runs registered updaters, ticks media children, recomputes Flex layouts, and batchDraws when active.

nodes.name: getComposition
nodes.summary: Returns the owning Composition for a Konva stage when it is a Composition instance, else null.

nodes.name: easing.ts
nodes.summary: Easing function library (linear, quad/cubic/poly, sin, circle, exp, elastic, back, bounce, bezier, steps) plus in/out/inOut combinators, exported as a single Easing object for use with interpolate.

nodes.name: emitter.ts
nodes.summary: Tiny typed event emitter factory used by Composition for composition-level lifecycle events (play, stop, frame, etc.).

nodes.name: createEmitter
nodes.summary: Creates a typed emitter with on/off/emit backed by a Map of event names to listener sets, returning unsubscribe handles from on().

nodes.name: environment.ts
nodes.summary: Runtime environment detection that distinguishes preview (browser) from rendering modes, allowing media subsystems to pick the appropriate driver.

nodes.name: detectEnvironment
nodes.summary: Determines the current Environment/EnvironmentMode, honoring an optional override, for branching between preview and rendering behavior.

nodes.name: getEnvironment
nodes.summary: Resolves the Environment associated with a given stage, falling back to detectEnvironment().

nodes.name: flex-engine.ts
nodes.summary: Thin adapter over the flexily flexbox library: parses CSS-like size/edge/alignment values, applies container and child flex props onto FlexilyNodes, and installs measure functions for text and image leaves.
nodes.languageNotes: Wraps flexily (synchronous JS flexbox, no WASM/async init) and re-exports FlexilyNode.

nodes.name: normalizeEdges
nodes.summary: Normalizes a scalar or array edge value into a uniform per-side {top,right,bottom,left} representation.

nodes.name: parseSize
nodes.summary: Parses a SizeValue into a pixel number or percentage descriptor, used widely by layout primitives.

nodes.name: applyContainerProps
nodes.summary: Applies flex container props (direction, justify, align, gap, padding) onto a FlexilyNode.

nodes.name: applyChildProps
nodes.summary: Applies flex child props (grow, shrink, basis, alignSelf, margin) onto a FlexilyNode.

nodes.name: applySize
nodes.summary: Sets width/height (pixel or percent) on a FlexilyNode from parsed SizeValues.

nodes.name: setTextMeasure
nodes.summary: Installs a flexily measure function for a Konva Text leaf, measuring intrinsic width/height honoring wrapping and column context.

nodes.name: setTextWrapperMeasure
nodes.summary: Installs a flexily measure function for the Text wrapper component, delegating to its _measureForFlex.

nodes.name: setImageMeasure
nodes.summary: Installs a flexily measure function for an Image leaf based on the loaded image's natural dimensions.

nodes.name: flex-types.ts
nodes.summary: Shared type definitions for flex layout, blocks, images, and text (Align, Justify, FlexConfig, BlockConfig, ImageConfig, TextConfig, gradient/shadow/edge value types, etc.).

nodes.name: flex.ts
nodes.summary: Flex container primitive (extends a Konva group) plus the recursive layout pipeline (layoutRoot/buildChildren/writeBack) that maps the Konva tree into flexily nodes, calculates layout, and writes computed positions/sizes back onto Konva nodes.
nodes.languageNotes: Builds a transient FlexilyNode tree each pass, calls calculateLayout, writes back, then freeRecursive to release native nodes.

nodes.name: Flex
nodes.summary: Konva-group-derived flex container whose computeLayout delegates to layoutRoot to reflow its subtree.

nodes.name: layoutRoot
nodes.summary: Builds the flexily root for a Konva group, applies container props, recursively constructs children, calculates layout, writes results back, and frees the native tree.

nodes.name: buildChildren
nodes.summary: Recursively maps visible Konva children into FlexilyNodes, dispatching on node kind (Flex, Block, Text wrapper/leaf, Image, plain) to apply props, sizes, and measure functions.

nodes.name: writeBack
nodes.summary: Copies computed flexily positions and sizes back onto paired Konva nodes and triggers per-node relayout hooks (_layoutBackground/_layoutImage/_layoutText).

nodes.name: image.ts
nodes.summary: Image layout primitive (extends a Konva group) that loads an HTMLImageElement, applies object-fit/object-position cropping, and clips to rounded corners.

nodes.name: Image
nodes.summary: Konva-group-derived image node that owns an inner Konva.Image, loads its source asynchronously, and recomputes fit/position/crop on layout.

nodes.name: loadImage
nodes.summary: Loads an image URL into an HTMLImageElement, resolving when decoded.

nodes.name: positionOffset
nodes.summary: Computes the x/y crop offset for an object-position keyword given the free space on each axis.

nodes.name: roundRectPath
nodes.summary: Draws a rounded-rectangle path on a canvas context for per-corner radius clipping.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Public barrel for @konva-motion/core, re-exporting the engine (Composition, Sequence), layout primitives (Flex, Block, Image, Text), animation math (interpolate, interpolateColors, Easing), media (Audio, Video), signals, and environment helpers.

nodes.name: interpolate-colors.ts
nodes.summary: Color interpolation utility that parses hex/rgb/rgba/hsl colors and interpolates each RGBA channel across an input range via the numeric interpolate function.

nodes.name: parseColor
nodes.summary: Parses a CSS color string (hex shorthand/full, rgb/rgba, hsl) into normalized RGBA components.

nodes.name: hslToRgb
nodes.summary: Converts HSL color components to RGB.

nodes.name: interpolateColors
nodes.summary: Interpolates an input value across an array of color stops, parsing each and blending RGBA channels with the numeric interpolate primitive.

nodes.name: interpolate.ts
nodes.summary: Core numeric interpolation primitive that maps an input value across input/output ranges with per-segment easing and configurable extrapolation.

nodes.name: interpolate
nodes.summary: Maps input across multi-point input/output ranges, selecting the active segment and applying easing and extrapolation rules.

nodes.name: interpolateSegment
nodes.summary: Interpolates a value within a single segment between two range points, applying easing and clamp/extend/identity extrapolation at the edges.

nodes.name: media-marker.ts
nodes.summary: Defines string marker attribute keys (MEDIA_MARK, VIDEO_MARK, AUDIO_MARK, TICK_MARK) used to tag and detect media nodes and tickable children on the Konva tree.

nodes.name: media-time.ts
nodes.summary: Maps a sequence-local frame to a media playback time (seconds) honoring trim-before/after and loop settings via getMediaTime.

nodes.name: getMediaTime
nodes.summary: Computes the media time in seconds for a local frame given MediaTiming (fps, trim, loop), clamping to valid bounds.

nodes.name: signal.ts
nodes.summary: Minimal reactive signal primitive: createSignal for read/write signals with subscription, and derived for computed signals over multiple sources.

nodes.name: createSignal
nodes.summary: Creates a writable signal with get/set/subscribe, notifying listeners only on actual value changes (Object.is).

nodes.name: derived
nodes.summary: Creates a read-only signal computed from multiple source signals, recomputing and notifying when any source changes.

nodes.name: text.ts
nodes.summary: Rich Text layout primitive (extends a Konva group) with auto-fit sizing, line clamping, highlight rendering, typewriter/character reveal animation, and a blinking cursor, integrated with the frame clock via _kmTick.
nodes.languageNotes: Builds per-character geometry and word-boundary indices to support word-snapped reveals and segment highlight rectangles.

nodes.name: Text
nodes.summary: Konva-group-derived text node managing an inner Konva.Text plus highlight/clip/fade/cursor layers; handles fit-to-box sizing, line clamping, highlight segments, frame-driven reveal, and caret positioning.

nodes.name: buildWordIndex
nodes.summary: Builds per-character word-end and word-membership indices used to snap character reveals to word boundaries.

nodes.name: driver.ts
nodes.summary: Video driver interface/types (VideoDriver, VideoDriverContext, VideoTiming) and a re-export of getMediaTime, shared by the preview and rendering video drivers.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the Video node (extends a Konva container) rendering a video frame with object-fit/crop and rounded corners, wiring audio to the composition mixer and selecting a preview or rendering VideoDriver by environment.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses requestVideoFrameCallback-driven sources and lazily resolves the Composition/Environment to choose the right driver.

nodes.name: Video
nodes.summary: Konva-group-derived video node owning an inner Konva.Image fed by a VideoSource; registers with the mixer, ticks a VideoDriver each frame, and applies fit/position/crop and audio settings.

nodes.name: isVideoNode
nodes.summary: Type guard detecting whether a Konva node is a Video node via its VIDEO_MARK attribute.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Type definitions for video configuration (VideoConfig) consumed by the Video node.

nodes.name: video-for-preview.ts
nodes.summary: PreviewVideoDriver: drives a video source for interactive playback, syncing native playback while playing and seeking the element when the playhead drifts from the expected media time.

nodes.name: PreviewVideoDriver
nodes.summary: Video driver for preview mode that subscribes to composition play state, seeks to the expected media time on drift, and plays/pauses the underlying element.

nodes.name: video-for-rendering.ts
nodes.summary: RenderingVideoDriver: deterministic offline driver that seeks the video to the exact media time per frame, using delayRender/continueRender to block rendering until the seek completes.

nodes.name: RenderingVideoDriver
nodes.summary: Video driver for offline rendering that seeks to the exact media time each tick and gates frame completion via the composition's delayRender/continueRender handles.

nodes.name: video-source-browser.ts
nodes.summary: BrowserVideoSource: wraps an HTMLVideoElement as a VideoSource, exposing load/seek/play/pause and frame callbacks via requestVideoFrameCallback with serialized seek draining.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses requestVideoFrameCallback and fastSeek where available, queuing/draining overlapping seek requests.

nodes.name: BrowserVideoSource
nodes.summary: VideoSource implementation backed by an HTMLVideoElement: loads media, serializes seeks, emits ready/frame callbacks, and exposes playback/volume controls.

nodes.name: video-source.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the runtime-agnostic VideoSource interface and VideoSourceFactory type — the dependency-injection seam between the animation engine and the underlying media decoder (browser HTMLVideoElement or server-side decoder).
nodes.languageNotes: Type-only module: exports a TypeScript interface plus two type aliases (SeekMode, VideoSourceFactory) with no runtime code. The `.js` import specifier targets the sibling `environment.ts` under NodeNext-style ESM resolution.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript project configuration for the @konva-motion/core package, extending the root config with composite build settings and an include pointing at src.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Package manifest for @konva-motion/timeline, the planned React UI components package (scrubber, play button); currently a placeholder declaring exports, types, and build scripts pointing at dist.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Placeholder entry point for the @konva-motion/timeline package, exporting only a TIMELINE_PACKAGE marker constant until the planned React UI components land.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript project configuration for the @konva-motion/timeline package, extending the root config with composite build settings and an include pointing at src.

nodes.name: findDemo
nodes.summary: Looks up a StudioDemo entry by id in the flat demo catalog, returning undefined when not found.

nodes.name: kmItemDefault
nodes.summary: Derives a default value for an array item schema via kmDefault, deep-cloning object defaults so each item is independent.

layers.name: Core Engine
layers.description: The @konva-motion/core animation engine: Composition/Sequence frame clock, Konva-extending nodes (Block, Flex, Image, Text), the flexily-backed layout engine, and animation math (easing, interpolation, signals, emitter, environment shims) plus the timeline placeholder package.

layers.name: Audio & Video Media Subsystem
layers.description: The audio and video subsystems of core: mixers, sources, drivers, assets, and preview/rendering variants that sync media playback to the frame clock for both browser preview and offline rendering.

layers.name: Demo Examples
layers.description: Animation example builders in demo/src/demos that exercise the core API (flex, text, image, audio/video, keyframes, easings) plus the demo entry HTML, bootstrap main.tsx, and asset/node shim glue.

layers.name: Studio UI
layers.description: The React-based studio interface (App, Player, Sidebar, RightPanel, SchemaForm, ZoomControl, Icon) with its composition/signal hooks, demo catalog, keyframe helpers, and styles for interactively previewing and scrubbing compositions.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project documentation including the root README, agent-facing CLAUDE.md, and the doc/ design, architecture, and contributing guides.

layers.name: Build & Project Config
layers.description: Workspace and per-package build/tooling configuration: pnpm workspace and npmrc, root and package tsconfig project references, Biome lint/format config, package manifests, Claude launch settings, and the demo's Vite config.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand what konva-motion is: a Remotion-style, timeline-driven animation layer built on top of Konva. It introduces the core mental model — a Composition that owns a frame clock and Sequences that gate animation to a frame range — with a runnable example, the package table (core, layout, timeline), and quick-start commands. Read this first to get the vocabulary the rest of the tour builds on.

tour.title: Engine Architecture
tour.description: The architecture doc explains the design decisions behind the engine before you read the code. It covers the core-vs-timeline package split, the per-frame tick loop that gates and redraws sequences, teardown, and singleton enforcement. Pair this with the agent-facing CLAUDE.md notes for the same mental model in condensed form.

tour.title: Core Public API
tour.description: packages/core/src/index.ts is the public barrel for @konva-motion/core and the single most depended-upon file in the project (every demo imports from it). It re-exports the engine (Composition, Sequence), the layout primitives (Flex, Block, Image, Text), the animation math (interpolate, interpolateColors, Easing), the media nodes (Audio, Video), and signal/environment helpers. Reading this gives you a one-screen map of everything the engine exposes.
tour.languageLesson: A TypeScript barrel file uses 're-export' syntax (export * / export { X } from './x') to collect many internal modules behind one import path, defining the package's public API surface in a single place.

tour.title: The Frame Clock: Composition & Sequence
tour.description: This is the heart of the engine. Composition extends Konva.Stage and owns the frame clock (fps, durationInFrames, reactive signals) driving a requestAnimationFrame tick loop, while Sequence extends Konva.Layer and is range-gated — its updaters run and it batchDraws only while the playhead sits inside [from, from + durationInFrames). Everything from Step 3's barrel ultimately serves this tick loop. Note the agnostic-runtime design: setFrame(n) works offline for server rendering, while play() is browser-only.
tour.languageLesson: The engine reads requestAnimationFrame/performance.now off globalThis with fallbacks so importing in Node is safe — a common pattern for runtime-agnostic libraries that must work in both browser and headless/server contexts.

tour.title: Reactivity & Events
tour.description: Building on the Composition's frame clock from Step 4, these primitives carry its state outward. signal.ts is a minimal reactive store (createSignal for read/write signals with subscriptions, derived for computed values) that the Composition exposes for frame/play state, and emitter.ts is a tiny typed event bus for composition lifecycle events (play, stop, frame). The studio UI later subscribes to these signals to render tear-free transport controls.

tour.title: Animation Math: Interpolate & Easing
tour.description: These are the functions you actually call inside a Sequence updater to compute values from the current frame. interpolate maps an input value across input/output ranges with per-segment easing and configurable extrapolation; interpolate-colors parses hex/rgb/hsl and interpolates each RGBA channel; easing.ts supplies the curve library (quad, cubic, elastic, bounce, bezier, steps) plus in/out/inOut combinators. Together they turn the raw frame counter from Step 4 into smooth motion.

tour.title: Built-in Flexbox Layout
tour.description: Layout is built into the engine so animated sizes and gaps reflow automatically each tick. flex.ts is the Flex container (extends Konva.Group) plus the recursive layout pipeline that maps the Konva tree into flexily nodes, computes layout, and writes positions/sizes back; flex-engine.ts is the thin adapter over the flexily library that parses CSS-like values and installs measure functions; flex-types.ts holds the shared Align/Justify/FlexConfig vocabulary. flex.ts and block.ts are tightly coupled — Blocks are the most common Flex children.

tour.title: Layout Primitives: Block, Image, Text
tour.description: These are the drawable leaves you place inside a Flex (from Step 7), each extending Konva.Group. Block renders a flex-laid-out box with solid/gradient fills, per-edge borders, corner radius, and shadows; Image loads an HTMLImageElement and applies object-fit/object-position cropping with rounded-corner clipping; Text is the richest, with auto-fit sizing, line clamping, highlight rendering, and a frame-driven typewriter/character-reveal animation wired into the tick loop via _kmTick.

tour.title: Media Subsystem: Audio & Video
tour.description: Audio and Video extend Konva containers and are range-gated to their sequence just like everything else, but they add a driver abstraction so playback works in two modes. environment.ts detects preview (browser) vs rendering; each subsystem picks a driver accordingly — preview drivers sync a live HTMLAudio/Video element to the playhead, while rendering drivers mute/seek deterministically per frame for offline export. media-time.ts maps a sequence-local frame to a media time honoring trim and loop, and the AudioMixer provides a per-composition master volume/mute bus.
tour.languageLesson: The driver/source split (AudioDriver, VideoSource, VideoSourceFactory) is dependency injection: the engine codes against an interface and the concrete browser-vs-rendering implementation is swapped at runtime by environment, keeping core decoupled from any specific media backend.

tour.title: Using the Engine: Demo Examples
tour.description: Now see the public API consumed in practice. basic.ts is the minimal introduction — two Sequences animating a circle's position and a square's opacity via updaters. keyframes.ts drives multi-stop position/rotation/scale/color animation (Step 6's interpolate in action), flex-showcase.ts exercises the layout engine from Steps 7-8, and cohabit.ts is a full promotional video orchestrating clips, music, voiceover, captions, and a logo reveal across many timed sequences using the media subsystem from Step 9. All demos share the DemoDef contract in types.ts.

tour.title: The React Studio
tour.description: The demo ships an interactive studio for previewing and scrubbing compositions. catalog.ts is the central registry importing every demo and attaching metadata/schemas; App.tsx wires routing, composition lifecycle, keyboard shortcuts, and the player/sidebar/right-panel layout; Player.tsx is the transport bar (play/pause, scrub, volume, fullscreen) bound to the composition's signals. The bridge from engine to React is the two hooks: useComposition builds and tears down a demo's Composition, and useSignal subscribes components to core ReadonlySignals.
tour.languageLesson: useSignal subscribes via React's useSyncExternalStore — the canonical hook for reading mutable external stores (here, the engine's signals) without tearing during concurrent rendering.

tour.title: Monorepo Build & Tooling
tour.description: Finally, the wiring that holds the workspace together. The root package.json and pnpm-workspace.yaml define the pnpm workspace where cross-package deps use workspace:*; tsconfig.json is a solution-style file referencing each package's composite build (tsc -b per package, emit to dist/). packages/core/package.json declares konva as a peer dependency (consumers pin the version) with flexily as a regular dep, and demo/vite.config.ts builds the studio app. This explains why editing core/src requires a rebuild before the demo sees the change.
tour.languageLesson: A peerDependency declares a package the consumer must supply (here konva), avoiding duplicate/mismatched copies — used for plugin-style libraries that extend a host framework's classes.
